---
title: "clean_flourish"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-12"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(flextable)

```

```{r}

mrp_preds <- read_csv(here("data","dec2025_mrp_preds.csv"))

ge_2024 <- read.csv("https://mrp-psf.ams3.digitaloceanspaces.com/other-files/ge2024_results.csv") 



mrp_preds <- mrp_preds %>%
  left_join(ge_2024 %>% select(const_name, winner, region), by = "const_name") %>%
  rename(ge_2024_winner = winner)

```

```{r}

## prepare for flourish 


mrp_preds <- mrp_preds %>% 
  rename(Green = "The Green Party", 
         SNP = "Scottish National Party (SNP)", 
         "Liberal Democrats" = "Liberal Democrat")


party_cols <- c("Conservative", "Labour", "Liberal Democrats", "Other", 
                "Plaid Cymru", "Reform UK", "Green", 
                "SNP")

mrp_results_flourish <- mrp_preds %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(party_cols), ~replace_na(., 0))) %>%
  select(-any_of("WNV")) %>%
  # Rescale to sum to 1
  mutate(
    row_sum = rowSums(across(all_of(party_cols))),
    across(all_of(party_cols), ~ . / row_sum)
  ) %>%
  select(-row_sum) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    new_winner = party_cols[which.max(c_across(all_of(party_cols)))]
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    change = case_when(
      new_winner == ge_2024_winner ~ paste(new_winner, "HOLD"),
      new_winner != ge_2024_winner ~ paste(new_winner, "GAIN from", ge_2024_winner),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

options(scipen = 999)
        
mrp_results_flourish <- mrp_results_flourish %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(party_cols), ~round(., 6)))




mrp_results_flourish <- mrp_results_flourish %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ . * 100))




write.csv(here(mrp_results_flourish, ))


table(mrp_results_flourish$new_winner)

table(mrp_results_flourish$change)



table(mrp_results_flourish$change)


write.csv(mrp_results_flourish, here("results", "overall_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)



```

```{r}

# Function to process MRP predictions and calculate vote distributions across different groupings
## default is remove would not vote - if want to have differneital tunrout switch order in rbackers in line 112

make_preds <- function(mrp_predictions, group_var, wnv = c("remove", "turnout"), ...) {
  
  # Match the wnv argument to either "turnout" or "remove"
  w <- match.arg(wnv)
  
  # If "remove" is selected, redistribute votes excluding WNV (Would Not Vote/abstentions) take them out and renomalize
  if (w == "remove") {
    mrp_predictions <- mrp_predictions %>% 
      select(-WNV) %>% 
      pivot_longer(Conservative:`Scottish National Party (SNP)`,
                   names_to = "party", values_to = "vote") %>%
      group_by(const_name) %>% 
      # Renormalize vote shares to sum to 1 after removing WNV
      mutate(vote = round(vote / sum(vote, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 2)) %>% 
      pivot_wider(names_from = "party", values_from = "vote")
    
  } else {
    mrp_predictions <- mrp_predictions %>% 
      mutate(turnout = 1 - WNV) %>% 
      select(-WNV) %>% 
      mutate(across(Conservative:`Scottish National Party (SNP)`, ~ .x * turnout))  %>% 
      pivot_longer(Conservative:`Scottish National Party (SNP)`,
                   names_to = "party", values_to = "vote") %>%
      group_by(const_name) %>% 
      mutate(vote = round(vote / sum(vote, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 2)) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = "party", values_from = "vote") %>%
      select(-turnout)
  }
  
  # Calculate the joint distribution: average vote shares by grouping variable and region
  joint_dist <- mrp_predictions %>%
    group_by(across(any_of(c(group_var, "region")))) %>% 
    summarise(
      across(Conservative:`Scottish National Party (SNP)`,
             ~ round(mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), 2)),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Calculate within-party distribution
  within_party <- joint_dist %>% 
    pivot_longer(-c(all_of(group_var), "region"),
                 names_to = "party", values_to = "vote") %>%
    group_by(region, party) %>% 
    mutate(vote = round(vote / sum(vote, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 2)) %>% 
    pivot_wider(names_from = "party", values_from = "vote")
  
  # Calculate within-group distribution
  within_group <- joint_dist %>% 
    mutate(
      row_total = rowSums(pick(Conservative:`Scottish National Party (SNP)`), na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      across(Conservative:`Scottish National Party (SNP)`, ~ round(.x / row_total * 100, 2))
    ) %>%
    select(-row_total)
  
  return(
    list(
      joint_dist   = joint_dist,
      within_party = within_party,
      within_group = within_group
    )
  )
}

## joint is the proportion of voters that have the group attribute _and_ vote for the party. 

## Within-party is the proportion of votes that come to that party from each group - depends on the distribution of the groups in the population
## these sum to 1 within the column (by region).

## within-group is the proportion of votes for each party from members of that group. 
## These sum to 1 in the row. 

## Both the within-group and within-party summaries are derived from the joint distribution.  The joint distribution will depend on the distirbution
## of group attributes in the population. E.g., if there are more people without degrees in the population, the shares in the non-degree group are going 
## to be bigger.  This generates some occasionally counter-intuitive looking results in the within-party analysis.  This is not a concern in the within-group
## analysis because the shares of the demographic group in the population do not enter into the calculation.  
```

```{r}


const_to_region <- read.csv("https://mrp-psf.ams3.digitaloceanspaces.com/other-files/Const%20to%20region.csv") %>% 
  mutate(region = case_when(region == "Scotland" ~ "Scotland", region == "Wales" ~ "Wales", TRUE ~ "England"))


```



```{r}
## Education = within group


mrp_preds_edu <- read_csv("degree/dec2025_mrp_preds.csv") %>% 
  left_join(const_to_region) 


 edu_aggregate <- make_preds(mrp_preds_edu, "edu_level")
 
  View(edu_aggregate$joint_dist)
  View(edu_aggregate$within_party)
  View(edu_aggregate$within_group)

  
     
arrange(edu_aggregate$within_group, region, edu_level)


  
 edu_within_group <- edu_aggregate$within_group %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  arrange(region, edu_level)
 
 
 
write.csv(edu_within_group, here("results", "education_within.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


ft <- flextable(edu_within_group)

save_as_docx(ft, path = here("results", "education_within.docx"))
 

```


```{r}

## within group age 

mrp_preds_age <- read_csv("age/dec2025_mrp_preds.csv") %>% 
  left_join(const_to_region) 


 age_aggregate <- make_preds(mrp_preds_age, "age_level")
 
  View(age_aggregate$joint_dist)
  View(age_aggregate$within_party)
  View(age_aggregate$within_group)


  arrange(age_aggregate$within_group, region, age_level)
  
  
  age_within_group <- age_aggregate$within_group %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  arrange(region, age_level)
  
  
write.csv(age_within_group, here("results", "age_within.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  

```

```{r}
## age sex


mrp_preds_age_sex <- read_csv("age_sex/dec2025_mrp_preds.csv") %>% 
  left_join(const_to_region)

age_sex_aggregate <- make_preds(
  mrp_predictions = mrp_preds_age_sex,
  group_var       = c("age_level", "sex_level")
)

# Drop the total component if make_preds() returns it
age_sex_aggregate$total <- NULL

View(age_sex_aggregate$joint_dist)
View(age_sex_aggregate$within_party)
View(age_sex_aggregate$within_group)

 
age_sex_aggregate$within_group <- age_sex_aggregate$within_group %>%
  arrange(region, age_level, sex_level)

  
  age_sex_within_group <- age_sex_aggregate$within_group %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  arrange(region, age_level, sex_level)
  
  
  
  
write.csv(age_sex_within_group, here("results", "age_sex_within.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  





```

```{r}

## ethnicity
  
 
 mrp_preds_ethn <- read_csv("ethnicity/dec2025_mrp_preds.csv") %>% 
left_join(const_to_region)


ethnic_aggregate <- make_preds(mrp_preds_ethn, "ethn_level")
 
  View(ethnic_aggregate$joint_dist)
  View(ethnic_aggregate$within_party)
  View(ethnic_aggregate$within_group)


  arrange(ethnic_aggregate$within_group, region, ethn_level)
  
  
  ethnic_within_group <- ethnic_aggregate$within_group %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  arrange(region, ethn_level)
  


  
write.csv(ethnic_within_group, here("results", "ethnicity_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
